sections.title: Total Realized Revenue
sections.content: This section captures the headline financial performance for the period. Understanding the aggregate realized revenue sets the baseline for evaluating whether the studio is trending toward its monthly targets. Any gap between expected and actual revenue should prompt a review of cancellations, refunds, or underperforming offerings.
sections.sql: SELECT COUNT(payment_id) AS total_transactions, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS total_revenue_dollars, ROUND(AVG(amount_dollars), 2) AS avg_transaction_value, ROUND(MIN(amount_dollars), 2) AS min_transaction, ROUND(MAX(amount_dollars), 2) AS max_transaction FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) >= '2026-04-20' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) <= '2026-05-20'

sections.title: Revenue Breakdown by Source
sections.content: Breaking revenue down by source reveals which product lines are carrying the business. Memberships typically anchor recurring revenue, while bookings and products reflect transactional activity. If memberships are underrepresented, it may signal churn risk or missed upsell opportunities; an oversized share from one-off bookings suggests the studio has room to convert more drop-ins into members.
sections.sql: SELECT purchasable_type AS source, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS revenue FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) >= '2026-04-20' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) <= '2026-05-20' GROUP BY purchasable_type ORDER BY revenue DESC

sections.title: Daily Revenue Trends
sections.content: Daily revenue trends expose volatility, peak days, and any prolonged dips across the 30-day window. Consistent peaks on certain days may correlate with popular class schedules or promotions, while troughs can point to scheduling gaps or demand shortfalls. Identifying these patterns helps the studio plan flash promotions or targeted communications to smooth out low-revenue days.
sections.sql: SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS daily_revenue FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) >= '2026-04-20' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) <= '2026-05-20' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

sections.title: Top Revenue-Generating Items
sections.content: Knowing which specific memberships, classes, or products generate the most revenue directs investment toward high-performers and highlights underutilized offerings. Items with high transaction counts but low average value may be strong volume drivers, while high-value items with few transactions represent upsell opportunities. This breakdown is essential for pricing reviews and promotional planning.
sections.sql: SELECT item_name, purchasable_type AS type, COUNT(payment_id) AS transactions, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS total_revenue, ROUND(AVG(amount_dollars), 2) AS avg_value FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) >= '2026-04-20' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) <= '2026-05-20' AND item_name IS NOT NULL AND item_name != '' GROUP BY item_name, purchasable_type ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

sections.title: Recommendations
sections.content: 1. **Shore up recurring revenue:** If memberships are not the dominant revenue source, prioritize converting frequent drop-in bookers with a targeted membership offer — even a discounted first month can shift the revenue mix toward more predictable income. 2. **Activate low-traffic days:** Use the daily trend chart to identify consistently weak days and schedule promotions, workshops, or limited-time discounts to drive bookings on those dates. 3. **Double down on top-performing items:** The highest-revenue classes and membership plans should receive more marketing visibility — feature them in emails, social posts, and the booking homepage to accelerate their contribution. 4. **Review low-volume, low-revenue items:** Items that appear in the bottom of the revenue table may be candidates for repricing, bundling, or discontinuation to simplify the offering and reduce operational overhead.
